source.journal_name: Romanian Journal of Information Science and Technology

paper.title: Low Temperature Co-Fired Ceramic Antenna for 35 GHz Applications with a Wideband GCPW to Stripline Transition
paper.abstract: Design, EM simulation and experimental results of 35 GHz LTCC antenna and GCPW-to-stripline transition. Six-tape LTCC process. Back-to-back transitions show reflection losses lower than -10 dB between 27-46.7 GHz, insertion losses lower than 1.5 dB. Antenna element has four parasitic microstrip patches with simulated directivity of 8.2 dBi at 35 GHz.

verdict.summary: RF/microwave engineering paper with appropriate formalism. EM simulations, S-parameter analysis, and experimental validation of LTCC antenna and transition design. All equations are domain-standard and necessary.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** LTCC antenna and GCPW-to-stripline transition design for 35 GHz
**Level:** 3 (RF/microwave engineering)
**Rationale:** Novel antenna element design with EM simulation and experimental validation. Requires standard RF engineering formalism.

### Formal Elements
- Definitions: 0
- Theorems: 0
- Proofs: 0
- Equations: ~5 (S-parameters, characteristic impedance, field distributions)

### Proportionality
Calculated complexity: ~10
Expected range (Level 3): 0-40
Ratio: 0.25x upper bound

**Assessment:** Proportionate - RF engineering requires S-parameter analysis, impedance matching equations, and EM field characterization.

### Necessity Test Applied
- S-parameter equations: Could be said in English? NO - precise dB values needed. Necessary for RF characterization.
- Characteristic impedance (54 Ohm GCPW, 35 Ohm SL): Could be said in English? NO - numerical design constraints. Necessary for impedance matching.
- Field distribution visualization: Could be said in English? NO - spatial EM field behavior. Necessary for transition design.

**All formal elements pass the Necessity Test.**

### Issues
None identified.

---

## Citation Analysis

No self-citations (0/12 references). Reference list covers relevant prior work on LTCC, antennas, and mm-wave technology. Citations [8-10] properly reference prior related work on parasitic patch antennas.

---

## Structural Analysis

Well-structured paper with clear methodology, simulation results, and experimental validation. Fabricated devices measured with VNA. Limitations acknowledged regarding calibrated gain measurements not possible with available equipment.

---

## Conclusion

Solid RF engineering paper with appropriate formalism for antenna and transition design. Experimental validation confirms simulation results. Standard domain practice with no formalism theater.

paper.title: Development and Experimental Results on LTCC Packages for Acoustic Wave Sensing Devices
paper.abstract: Novel LTCC packages for SAW and FBAR sensing devices. DuPont 9K7 green tapes with 3-D EM optimization. CPW transmission line configuration for impedance matching. Linear frequency shift vs temperature/humidity demonstrated. SMT compatible packaging with Au wire bonding.

verdict.summary: RF packaging paper with appropriate formalism for impedance matching and package design. 3D EM simulation with CST Microwave Studio. Equations for equivalent circuit extraction are standard and necessary.

papers.provenance:

paper.title: POCl3 Annealing Effect on The Flat Band Voltage Instabilities for a SiC Based MOS Capacitor at High Temperature
paper.abstract: Post-oxidation annealing (POA) effects on SiC MOS capacitor for high temperature applications. POCl3 ambient POA reduces flat band voltage instabilities. C-V measurements up to 623K. POA samples show improved VFB stability compared to as-oxidized structures.

verdict.summary: Materials science paper on SiC MOS device characterization. Standard semiconductor physics equations for capacitance and flat-band voltage analysis. Appropriate formalism for device physics.

papers.provenance:

- Eq. 1 (active capacitance): Could be said in English? NO - mathematical relationship for parasitic removal. Necessary.
- Eq. 2 (flat-band capacitance): Could be said in English? NO - device physics. Necessary for CFB calculation.
- Eq. 3 (Debye length): Could be said in English? NO - fundamental semiconductor parameter. Necessary.
- Eq. 5 (1/C^2 analysis): Could be said in English? NO - Schottky-Mott method. Necessary for VFB extraction.
- Eq. 6 (VFB with oxide charges): Could be said in English? NO - device physics relationship. Necessary.

**All formal elements pass the Necessity Test.**

Clear experimental methodology. Fabrication process described. C-V measurements at multiple temperatures. Comparative analysis of as-oxidized vs POA samples. Limitations section present (acknowledges need for further interface trap density studies).

---

## Conclusion

Solid semiconductor device characterization paper with appropriate formalism. Empirical results support POCl3 POA for improved high-temperature stability. No formalism theater.

paper.title: A CMOS Imager for Time-of-Flight and Photon Counting Based on Single Photon Avalanche Diodes and In-Pixel Time-to-Digital Converters
paper.abstract: 64x64 CMOS image sensor with SPAD array and in-pixel TDC for 3D imaging. 0.18um standard CMOS. Programmable time resolution down to 145 ps. DNL < 1 LSB, INL < 1.7 LSB. Both time-of-flight and photon counting modes. Frame rate up to 1 kfps.

verdict.summary: Mixed-signal IC design paper with novel SPAD-based ToF imager. Minimal formalism - one jitter equation. Extensive circuit-level description and measured results. Fabricated chip with comprehensive characterization.

paper.title: Integrated Dual-chain Dickson Charge Pump optimized for Resistive Loads
paper.abstract: Dual-chain Dickson charge pump with active and passive charge transfer switches. Sources up to 500 uA at 10 V output. RC oscillator with frequency matching to pumping capacitors. Automotive BCD technology. Designed for NMOS LDO regulators.

verdict.summary: Analog IC design paper with standard charge pump equations. Appropriate formalism for circuit design and analysis. Fabricated and measured chip in automotive BCD technology.

paper.title: Beam Shaping for Phase Singularities
paper.abstract: Optical vortex generation using spiral phase plates and fork-like CGHs. 635 nm wavelength. Reflective SPPs fabricated with photolithography/RIE. CGHs fabricated with EBL. Topological charges m=2, m=4 demonstrated. Michelson interferometer for phase singularity characterization.

verdict.summary: Optics paper on optical vortex generation. Equations for orbital angular momentum and Kirchhoff diffraction are standard physics and necessary for beam shaping analysis. Fabricated devices with structural and functional characterization.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

**Level:** 3 (Optics/photonics)

### Formal Elements

- Theorems: 0
- Proofs: 0

### Proportionality
Calculated complexity: ~12
Expected range (Level 3): 0-40
Ratio: 0.3x upper bound

**Assessment:** Proportionate - optical vortex theory requires wave equations and diffraction integrals.

### Necessity Test Applied
- Eqs. 1-2 (orbital angular momentum): Could be said in English? NO - fundamental physics. Necessary for OAM theory.
- Eq. 3 (photon angular momentum): Could be said in English? NO - quantum optics relationship. Necessary.
- Eq. 4 (Kirchhoff integral): Could be said in English? NO - diffraction calculation. Necessary for simulation.
- Eq. 5 (CGH formula): Could be said in English? NO - hologram calculation. Necessary for design.
- Eq. 6 (SPP height): Could be said in English? NO - design equation. Necessary for fabrication.

**All formal elements pass the Necessity Test.**

### Issues
None identified.

---

---

## Structural Analysis

Clear structure: simulation, fabrication, characterization. WLI for structural, optical measurements for functional. Michelson interferometer for topological charge detection. Good agreement between simulation and experiment.

---

## Conclusion

High-quality optics paper with appropriate theoretical formalism. Fabricated SPPs and CGHs with comprehensive characterization. No formalism theater.

paper.title: Dielectric Sensing with Back-Gated Nanowires
paper.abstract: Numerical study of back-gated nanowire capacitance for dielectric sensing. Effective dielectric constant scaling for various embeddings. ANSYS finite element simulations. S-shaped capacitance vs dielectric thickness with three distinct regions for sensing applications.

verdict.summary: Semiconductor physics paper on nanowire FET capacitance. Equations for FET mobility and capacitance are standard semiconductor device physics. Numerical simulation with ANSYS. Appropriate formalism for theoretical analysis.

paper.title: High Temperature Compensated Voltage Reference Integrated Circuits on 4H-SiC Material
paper.abstract: High temperature voltage reference IC on 4H-SiC MESFET technology. Temperature coefficient 15-33 ppm/C up to 300C. Bandgap reference concept analyzed with Schottky and bipolar diodes. Novel MESFET-based VR without OpAmp. Integrated power MESFET on same chip. Linear temperature sensor included.

verdict.summary: Analog IC design paper on SiC voltage reference. Standard semiconductor device equations for diode characteristics and bandgap reference. Novel circuit topology for SiC without OpAmp. Fabricated IC with measurements up to 300C.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** High-temperature compensated voltage reference IC on 4H-SiC
**Level:** 3 (Analog IC design on wide-bandgap semiconductor)
**Rationale:** Novel voltage reference circuit on SiC MESFET technology for harsh environment applications.

### Formal Elements
- Definitions: 0
- Theorems: 0
- Proofs: 0
- Equations: 5 (Eqs. 1-5 for diode current, voltage, and bandgap reference)

### Proportionality
Calculated complexity: ~10
Expected range (Level 3): 0-40
Ratio: 0.25x upper bound

**Assessment:** Proportionate - analog IC design requires device equations for circuit analysis.

### Necessity Test Applied
- Eqs. 1-2 (diode current): Could be said in English? NO - exponential I-V relationship. Necessary for design.
- Eq. 3 (diode voltage): Could be said in English? NO - forward voltage calculation. Necessary.
- Eq. 4 (PTAT voltage): Could be said in English? NO - thermal compensation design. Necessary.
- Eq. 5 (bandgap voltage): Could be said in English? NO - reference voltage combination. Necessary.

**All formal elements pass the Necessity Test.**

### Issues
None identified.

---

## Citation Analysis

Self-citation: 4/25 references ([1-2], [13-14] - prior related work on SiC ICs). Ratio ~16% - elevated but acceptable for follow-up work in emerging field. All self-citations are topically relevant.

---

## Structural Analysis

Comprehensive paper: bandgap theory, MESFET design, circuit schematic, simulation, fabrication, measurements. Temperature characterization up to 300C. PSRR measured. Application demonstration with power MESFET.

---

## Conclusion

High-quality analog IC paper with appropriate formalism. First reported SiC MESFET voltage reference. Experimental validation in harsh environment. No formalism theater.

batch_notes: Volume 17 Issue 4 (2014) analyzed with RUIN Framework v2.0 and Necessity Test. **No formalism theater detected.** All 8 papers are hardware/device engineering papers with domain-appropriate formalism: LTCC antenna, LTCC packages, SiC MOS devices, SPAD imagers, charge pumps, optical vortices, nanowire sensors, SiC voltage references. All equations pass the Necessity Test - they are standard domain equations necessary for design and analysis. Final: 0 CRITICAL, 0 LIMITED, 2 ADEQUATE, 6 STRONG.
